problem.statement:
Given an array of integers, sort it in **ascending order** using **Bucket Sort** and print the sorted array.

Print space-separated integers on one line.

Given an array of integers, sort it in **ascending order** using **Counting Sort** and print the sorted array.

Print space-separated integers on one line.

Given a multiset of `n` integers (it may contain duplicates), print the number of **distinct subsets** of it, counting the empty subset. Two subsets are the same if they contain the same multiset of values regardless of which original duplicate index they came from.

Given an array of integers, sort it in **ascending order** using **Gnome Sort** and print the sorted array.

Print space-separated integers on one line.
Given a binary tree, print `true` if it is **height-balanced** (every node's two subtree heights differ by at most 1), else `false`.
Given a **binary search tree** and an integer `k`, print the `k`-th smallest value in it (1-indexed).
Given a grid of 0s and 1s (at least one 0), print the **maximum, over all cells**, of the 4-directional distance from that cell to the nearest 0 cell.
problem.constraints: ["1 \u2264 rows, cols \u2264 20", "at least one cell is 0"]

problem.statement:
Given an array of integers, sort it in **ascending order** using **Merge Sort** and print the sorted array.

Print space-separated integers on one line.

Given an array of integers, sort it in **ascending order** using **Quick Sort** and print the sorted array.

Print space-separated integers on one line.
Given an `N x N` matrix, print the matrix **rotated 90 degrees clockwise**, one row per line, space-separated values.
Given an `R x C` matrix, if any cell equals `0`, set its entire row and entire column to `0`. Print the resulting matrix, one row per line, space-separated values.

Given `n` men and `n` women, each with a fully-ranked preference list over the other side (`men_prefs[i]`/`women_prefs[i]`, most preferred first), print the **man-optimal stable matching** produced by the Gale-Shapley algorithm (men propose): for each man `0..n-1`, print his assigned woman's index, space-separated.

Given an `rows x cols` grid of lowercase letters and a target `word`, print `true` if `word` can be built by moving between **orthogonally adjacent** cells (up, down, left, right) without reusing the same cell twice, else `false`.
